title: Hypothyroidism follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

medication.text: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.
medication.rationale: The exact hypothyroidism monitoring section supports interval change, persistent symptom context, clinician-reviewed thyroid results, an entered plan and arranged follow-up without changing levothyroxine or inferring biochemical control.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.6 — follow-up and monitoring of primary hypothyroidism

medication.text: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.
medication.rationale: The exact information section supports documenting patient goals, questions, discussed safety net and prior endocrine correspondence without inserting medicine instructions.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — information and thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms

source_limitations: Cause, symptom detail, medicine, administration, interactions, adherence, pregnancy, exact results, interpretation and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
